checks.finding: Deletion semantics (whole line + terminator, any field value, LF/CRLF, task-stage-only scope) are stated in exact operational language; round-2 added an explicit non-oracle note for the Doc Status column (acceptance-tests.md line 20), removing the prior ambiguity.

REQ-001's freeze goal and the Non-goal 'No retroactive re-hashing of existing task-review contracts' are consistent: frozen tasks.md never contains a Second Approval line at freeze time, so deletion is a no-op under old and new normalization alike. AC/TEST map 1:1; no direct conflict found.

requirements.md Edge Cases states multiple Second Approval lines (one per critical task) are all deleted, and Source Issues names the motivating scenario as 'epic-136-phase1-guards T-001/T-002' (two critical tasks in one tasks.md). acceptance-tests.md TEST-001..004 exercise only a single-line fixture per case — no AC/TEST-ID covers 2+ lines in one file, leaving the actual originating multi-critical-task scenario unverified end-to-end.
Both assumptions (pwsh 7.x, --registry entry point) are anchored to existing repo facts; Open Questions explicitly closed with inline justification.
Human-only authorship of the field is correctly scoped as unchanged; the human-only delivery boundary is now testable via the round-2 AC-005/TEST-005 SHA-256 live-vs-staged-MANIFEST gate evidence.

Exact algorithmic behavior, enumerated edge cases, security-boundary reasoning, roles/permissions, and workflow sequencing are all present; the one residual gap is a test-completeness issue captured under EDGE-CASE-COVERAGE, not missing behavior.
